title: Where does the database password live?

mcq.question_md: Your deployed notice board uses a Postgres database that needs a password. Where should that password live?
options: In the React code, so the frontend can query the database directly
In an environment variable on the backend host, read by Spring Boot at startup
Committed to GitHub in `application.properties`, so teammates can run the project
In the browser's localStorage, set once when the app first loads
mcq.explanation_md: Only the backend should ever hold secrets. The host (Render, for example) stores the password as an environment variable, Spring Boot reads it at startup, and it never appears in your repo or your bundle. Here's the rule behind it: **everything in a React app ships to the visitor's browser** — anyone can open DevTools and read your entire frontend code, which rules out options A and D completely. Option C is the tempting one because it feels team-friendly, but a password pushed to GitHub is public forever (even after you delete it, it lives in the git history). Teammates get a `application.properties.example` with blank values instead.
